sourceText: This emphasizes that humanity's essence lies in seeking truth, and knowledge is the origin of all understanding and progress.
All knowledge ultimately leads to one.
This signifies that even though various academic fields may appear different on the surface, deep exploration reveals their interconnectedness at fundamental principles or truths.
Observation is the first step of knowledge, and contemplation completes the path.
This means that carefully observing the world is the beginning of acquiring knowledge, and the process of deep thought and reflection on that acquired knowledge completes its understanding.
The entrenched dogma of Galen, the skepticism from the established scientific community towards novel ideas, and the arduous task of gathering irrefutable evidence for his theories.

Through meticulous dissection and experimentation, he presented undeniable proof that blood circulates from the heart, through the body, and back again. His persistent dedication and scientific inquiry eventually earned acceptance within the medical world.
The heart is the organ of the body, and the sun of the microcosm.
This signifies that the heart is the central organ of blood circulation, playing a crucial role in regulating all functions of a living organism.
This means that true learning is achieved through direct experience and experimentation, not just through theory.
Truth comes from observation and experiment.
This emphasizes the importance of scientific inquiry, stating that only through consistent observation of natural phenomena and rigorous experimentation can objective truth be revealed.

Priestley faced social condemnation and political persecution due to his radical religious and political views. His support for the French Revolution and his opposition to the Church of England led to the destruction of his church and home by a mob.
